title: fleurya spicata var. interrupta
intro: Fleurya spicata var. interrupta, also known as the interrupted fleurya or fleurya, is a perennial plant species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of fleurya include the potential treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Fleurya can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it's also used as a topical application for skin conditions and injuries.
